page: Google Cloud Platform c4d-highmem-4 is an x86_64 architecture server instance belonging to the c4d family. It features 4 dedicated virtual CPUs (vCPUs) paired with 31.0 GB of system memory, yielding a memory-to-core ratio of 7.75 GB per vCPU. This configuration does not include local storage or integrated GPU hardware, meaning all storage requirements must be met via network-attached block storage.

The instance is designed for memory-intensive workloads that do not require massive compute scaling. With 31.0 GB of RAM allocated to only 4 dedicated vCPUs, the server provides a high memory-to-vCPU density. This resource profile offers qualitative cost efficiency for applications that are constrained by memory capacity rather than raw processing power, allowing users to avoid provisioning excess vCPUs simply to acquire sufficient RAM.

Given its hardware specifications, the c4d-highmem-4 is suited for workloads such as medium-sized databases, caching layers, in-memory data processing, and enterprise applications with moderate CPU demands but high memory footprints. The lack of local storage and GPU acceleration makes it less suitable for high-performance local database logging or machine learning training workloads.
description: Google Cloud Platform c4d-highmem-4 is an x86_64 architecture server instance within the c4d family, configured with 4 dedicated vCPUs and 31.0 GB of RAM. This resource allocation provides a high memory-to-core ratio of 7.75 GB per vCPU, making it highly suitable for memory-constrained workloads. The instance does not include local storage or GPU accelerators, relying entirely on network-attached storage for data persistence. This hardware profile is qualitatively cost-efficient for applications requiring significant memory capacity without the need to scale up compute resources. It is well-suited for workloads such as in-memory caching, database hosting, and moderate data processing tasks that demand dedicated CPU allocation but do not require high-performance local disk I/O.

tagline: An x86_64 server configuration offering dedicated compute resources paired with a high memory-to-vCPU ratio for memory-intensive applications.

No integrated GPU or local storage included
Suitable for in-memory caching, databases, and general-purpose applications
